title: Crispy Ukrainian Potato Dumplings
description: Malty, crisp, vegan-friendly dumplings that stay al-dente inside while caramelized on the outside; ideal for meal‑prep.
instructions: Peel and grate the potatoes, then squeeze out excess moisture with a clean towel.
In a bowl, mix flour, caster sugar, salt, and baking powder.
Add the grated potato and cold water to the dry mixture, stirring until a firm dough forms.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each into a small disc about 1.5cm thick.
Bring a pot of salted water to a gentle boil, then reduce to a near‑simmer and cook the discs for 3 minutes, ensuring they stay al‑dente.
Drain the dumplings and pat them dry with paper towels.
Heat the vegetable oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Fry the dumplings in batches, turning until golden and caramelized, about 3 minutes per side.
Transfer cooked dumplings to a platter lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Serve warm, optionally sprinkled with a pinch of nutritional yeast for extra flavor.
